title: Early Life Exposure to Manure-Fertilized Soil Shapes the Gut Antibiotic Resistome.
ehr_evidence: None. This is an experimental animal study.
summary: An experimental study investigating how early life exposure to manure-fertilized soil (red and black soils) shapes the gut antibiotic resistome in mice.
key_findings: Manure fertilization significantly enriched tetracycline-resistant ARGs in both red and black soils.
Mice in manure-fertilized environments harbored markedly higher abundances of ARGs, particularly the tet(Q) gene.
Muribaculaceae and Bacteroidaceae were identified as key hosts of tet(Q).
Evidence suggests horizontal gene transfer between Muribaculaceae and Bacteroidaceae.
limitations: Animal model (mice) may not perfectly replicate human gut microbiome dynamics
data_availability_statement: The raw metagenomic sequence data are publicly available under the accession number PRJNA1206751.
